name: Mushroom Butterflies
recipeIngredient: Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
12 oz Butterfly or bow-tie pasta, uncooked
Coarsely grated Parmesan cheese, for garnish (optional)
recipeInstructions:
For a delicate dinner or lunch pasta, try my mushroom sauce tossed with bow-tie or butterfly shapes. Use extra-virgin olive oil for flavor when cooking this dish, as there are so few ingredients. It's not necessary to season the pasta with salt if using Parmesan cheese, as it's already salty. If you opt for Parmesan, freshly grated is recommended.
1. Trim mushroom stems and wipe caps clean with a paper towel. Slice lengthwise.
2. Toss mushrooms, shallots, and garlic with oil in a large, heavy saucepan. Cook over medium-high heat, stirring frequently, for 10 minutes. Season with pepper. Add 1/3 cup parsley and lemon zest. Cook for an additional minute. Reserve, covered, on the stove.
3. Shortly before serving, cook the pasta in boiling, salted water, stirring once, for 6-8 minutes or until al dente. Drain, reserving 2 tablespoons of the cooking liquid. Toss pasta with mushrooms and reserved liquid; then toss with Parmesan cheese and serve immediately, or serve the cheese alongside. Garnish with remaining tablespoon of chopped parsley.
